analytical.question: What recurring topics does the user's watch history reveal based on the most recently watched 50 videos?
analytical.answer: Two recurring themes: (1) kids cartoons and nursery rhymes (Ben 10, StoryBots, Wiggles, Disney Kids), and (2) media/cognition/bias topics (The Subtle Effects of Repetition, How Exposure Shapes Beliefs, Why People Gravitate Toward Familiar Ideas, Why small biases matter).

analytical.question: What polarizing patterns, if any, does the user's watch history reveal based on the least recently watched 50 videos?
analytical.answer: Repeated anti-liberal framing in titles, e.g., 'Why Liberals Destroy Everything Good', 'Why Liberals Hate Tradition', 'Why liberals keep missing this point', 'Why Liberals Destroy Culture', and 'Why Liberals Are Destroying Tradition'.

analytical.question: What political biases, if any, does the user's watch history reveal based on the the most recently watched 50 videos?
analytical.answer: No clear partisan political bias. The most recent 50 are dominated by kids cartoons/children's songs and general media/cognition commentary (e.g., The Role of Framing in Everyday News, Why Content Feeds Reinforce Views, The Gradual Shift in Online Discourse, Why Small Biases Matter Over Time).

analytical.question: Which health-related misinformation topics, if any, does the user's watch history reveal based on the least recently watched 50 videos?
analytical.answer: Vaccine misinformation, e.g., 'The Vaccine Hoax They Don't Want You to Know' and 'The Vaccine Truth Finally Revealed'.
